name: Pelvic Floor Physical Therapy
note: Specialized PT for pelvic floor dysfunction, pain, prolapse, and incontinence; prenatal and postpartum applications

name: Myofascial Release Physical Therapy
note: Hands-on manual therapy targeting myofascial restrictions causing pain and mobility limitation

name: Sports Physical Therapy
note: Injury rehabilitation and performance optimization for athletes and active individuals

name: Vestibular Physical Therapy
note: Specialized PT for dizziness, vertigo, BPPV, and balance disorders

name: TMJ Physical Therapy
note: Manual therapy and exercise for temporomandibular joint dysfunction, jaw pain, and related headaches

name: Aquatic Therapy
note: Exercise-based therapy performed in warm water pool; reduces joint load; good for pain and mobility

name: Occupational Therapy
note: Supports daily living activities, fine motor skills, sensory integration, and adaptive strategies

name: Speech Therapy
note: Swallowing, communication, voice, and language disorders; also tongue tie and feeding issues

name: Massage Therapy
note: General relaxation and therapeutic muscle work; reduces stress hormones and muscle tension

name: Deep Tissue Massage
note: Deeper pressure targeting chronic muscle tension; may cause soreness 1-2 days post-session

name: Lymphatic Drainage Massage
note: Very light pressure technique stimulating lymph flow; edema, post-surgical, detox, and immune support

name: Craniosacral Therapy
note: Extremely gentle technique assessing and releasing restrictions in craniosacral rhythm; trauma and headache focus

name: Rolfing
note: 10-session structural integration series reorganizing the body's fascia relative to gravity

name: Structural Integration
note: Broad category of deep connective tissue and movement education therapies derived from Rolfing

name: Chiropractic Adjustment
note: Spinal and joint manipulation; musculoskeletal pain; evidence strongest for low back pain

name: Osteopathic Manipulation
note: Full-body osteopathic manual therapy by a DO; includes soft tissue, counterstrain, and HVLA techniques

name: Reflexology
note: Pressure applied to foot, hand, or ear points believed to correspond to body organs; relaxation and wellness

name: Cupping Therapy
note: Suction cups applied to skin creating negative pressure; used for muscle tension, pain, and circulation

name: Gua Sha
note: Scraping skin with smooth tool to improve circulation; used in TCM and modern soft tissue therapy

name: Trigger Point Therapy
note: Direct pressure to muscle knots (trigger points) to release pain referral patterns

name: Acupuncture
note: Fine needles inserted at meridian points; well-studied for pain, fertility, and nausea

name: Acupressure
note: Manual pressure on acupuncture points without needles; self-care version also practical

name: Reiki
note: Hands-on or distant energy healing; promotes deep relaxation; evidence primarily subjective but widely reported

name: Healing Touch
note: Biofield therapy; gentle intentional touch used in hospital and integrative settings

name: Homeopathy
note: Highly diluted remedies matched to constitutional type; evidence debated; widely used in Europe

name: Naturopathic Consultation
note: Comprehensive integrative health consultation with licensed naturopathic doctor (ND/NMD)

name: Functional Medicine Consultation
note: Root-cause approach using advanced testing and lifestyle interventions; long-form consultations

name: Integrative Medicine Consultation
note: MD or DO combining conventional and complementary approaches within evidence-based framework

name: Ayurvedic Medicine
note: Traditional Indian medicine system; constitutional assessment (dosha), diet, herbs, and lifestyle

name: Traditional Chinese Medicine
note: Includes acupuncture, herbal medicine, cupping, and dietary therapy; qi and pattern-based diagnosis

name: Herbalist Consultation
note: Personalized herbal medicine protocol from trained herbalist; Western, Chinese, or Ayurvedic tradition

name: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy
note: Gold standard psychotherapy for anxiety, depression, OCD, and many conditions; structured, skills-based

name: EMDR
note: Trauma processing therapy using bilateral stimulation; strong evidence base for PTSD

name: Somatic Therapy
note: Body-oriented trauma therapy; Peter Levine SE, Pat Ogden Sensorimotor; discharges stored stress response

name: EFT Tapping
note: Tapping on acupressure points while addressing emotional issues; anxiety, trauma, and cravings

name: MBSR
note: Standardized 8-week mindfulness program; strong evidence for anxiety, pain, and chronic illness

name: Biofeedback
note: Real-time physiological data feedback to train self-regulation; stress, pain, and autonomic function

name: Neurofeedback
note: EEG-based brain training; used for ADHD, trauma, anxiety, and peak performance

name: Hypnotherapy
note: Guided hypnotic state for behavior change, pain, IBS, anxiety, and habit modification

name: Art Therapy
note: Therapeutic use of art-making; trauma, grief, and expression for those unable to verbalize

name: Music Therapy
note: Clinical use of music to address physical, emotional, and cognitive needs; evidence-based

name: Grief Counseling
note: Specialized counseling for grief and loss; includes complicated grief and anticipatory grief

name: Couples Counseling
note: Relationship therapy for communication, intimacy, and conflict; Gottman method widely validated

name: Group Therapy
note: Therapist-led group; interpersonal learning, universality, and social support; often more affordable

name: Psychiatry Consultation
note: MD/DO specializing in mental health; medication management and diagnosis; may include therapy

name: Endocrinologist Visit
note: Specialist for hormonal disorders: thyroid, diabetes, adrenal, pituitary, and reproductive hormones

name: Rheumatologist Visit
note: Specialist for autoimmune and inflammatory conditions: lupus, RA, Sjogren's, fibromyalgia

name: Gastroenterologist Visit
note: Specialist for digestive conditions: IBD, IBS, celiac, SIBO, and motility disorders

name: Neurologist Visit
note: Specialist for brain and nervous system conditions: migraines, neuropathy, epilepsy, MS

name: Cardiologist Visit
note: Specialist for heart and vascular conditions; preventive and interventional cardiology

name: Dermatologist Visit
note: Specialist for skin, hair, and nail conditions; annual skin checks for melanoma prevention

name: Gynecologist Visit
note: Annual wellness exam, Pap smear, STI screening, contraception, and hormonal health

name: Urologist Visit
note: Specialist for urinary tract and male reproductive system; pelvic floor issues, prostate health

name: Allergist Visit
note: Skin prick testing, food allergy evaluation, immunotherapy (allergy shots/drops)

name: Immunologist Visit
note: Specialist for immune system disorders; autoimmunity, immune deficiency, mast cell disorders

name: Hematologist Visit
note: Specialist for blood disorders: anemia, clotting disorders, hemophilia, and blood cancers

name: Oncologist Visit
note: Cancer diagnosis and treatment specialist; medical, surgical, and radiation oncology branches

name: Integrative Oncologist Visit
note: Combines conventional cancer treatment with evidence-based integrative approaches (supplements, acupuncture, etc.)

name: Naturopath Visit
note: Comprehensive consultation with licensed ND; emphasizes root-cause medicine and natural interventions

name: Pelvic Health Specialist
note: Combined medical and physical therapy approach to pelvic pain, prolapse, and dysfunction

name: Oil Pulling
note: Swish oil in mouth 15-20 minutes; Ayurvedic practice for oral microbiome and detox; do before eating

name: Dry Brushing
note: Natural bristle brush on dry skin before shower; exfoliation and lymphatic circulation stimulation

name: Epsom Salt Bath
note: 2 cups Epsom salts in warm bath; transdermal magnesium absorption and muscle relaxation

name: Contrast Shower
note: Alternating hot and cold water; stimulates circulation, lymphatic flow, and nervous system resilience

name: Near-Infrared Sauna
note: Near-infrared wavelengths penetrate tissue; mitochondrial and skin benefits; lower heat than traditional sauna

name: Far-Infrared Sauna
note: Far-infrared heat penetrates deeply; sweating and detoxification; cardiovascular conditioning benefit

name: Cold Plunge
note: Immersion in cold water (50-59F); reduces inflammation, improves mood, recovery, and resilience

name: Ice Bath
note: Post-exercise recovery in ice-cold water; reduces muscle soreness; timing relative to training matters

name: Earthing
note: Direct skin contact with earth (grass, soil, sand); proposed electron transfer and inflammation reduction

name: Halotherapy
note: Inhaling pharmaceutical-grade salt aerosol; respiratory, skin, and relaxation benefits reported

name: Float Therapy
note: Floating in Epsom salt solution in soundproof dark pod; deep relaxation, pain relief, and creativity

name: Red Light Therapy Home Device
note: 660-850nm wavelengths; skin, joint, and mitochondrial benefits; daily use for best results

name: IV Vitamin C
note: Intravenous high-dose vitamin C; immune support, post-illness recovery, and integrative cancer adjunct

name: IV Myers Cocktail
note: Classic IV blend: vitamin C, B vitamins, magnesium, calcium; fatigue, migraines, fibromyalgia

name: IV Glutathione
note: Intravenous glutathione; detoxification, immune support, and antioxidant repletion

name: IV NAD+
note: Intravenous NAD+; must be infused slowly; used for addiction recovery, brain fog, and aging

name: B12 Injection
note: Intramuscular or subcutaneous B12; bypasses absorption issues; cyanocobalamin or methylcobalamin forms

name: Iron IV Infusion
note: Intravenous iron for severe deficiency or malabsorption; faster repletion than oral supplementation

name: Hormone Pellet Insertion
note: Subdermal pellet containing testosterone or estradiol; released steadily over 3-6 months

name: PRP Injection
note: Autologous platelet-rich plasma injected into joints, scalp, or tissue; growth factors promote healing

name: Prolotherapy
note: Injection of irritant solution (dextrose) to stimulate ligament and tendon healing

name: Ozone Therapy IV
note: Blood drawn, mixed with ozone, reinfused; antimicrobial, immune, and oxygen utilization effects

name: Ozone Therapy Rectal
note: Ozone gas administered rectally; used for gut, systemic infection, and inflammatory conditions

name: HBOT
note: 100% oxygen at increased atmospheric pressure; wound healing, TBI, Lyme adjunct, anti-aging interest

name: Yoga
note: Integrates movement, breathwork, and meditation; broad evidence for stress, flexibility, and pain

name: Yin Yoga
note: Passive holds 3-5 minutes; targets deep connective tissue; complements more active practices

name: Restorative Yoga
note: Deeply passive props-supported poses; activates parasympathetic nervous system; ideal for adrenal fatigue

name: Kundalini Yoga
note: Combines movement, breathwork, chanting, and meditation; energizing and transformative practice

name: Pilates
note: Core stability and controlled movement; pelvic floor and postural support; classical and contemporary forms

name: Tai Chi
note: Slow meditative martial art; balance, joint health, and stress reduction; strong evidence in elderly

name: Qigong
note: Chinese energy movement practice; breathwork with gentle movements; stress, immunity, and energy

name: Dance Therapy
note: Creative movement as therapy; body image, trauma, emotion, and somatic awareness

name: Feldenkrais Method
note: Somatic learning using gentle movement; reprograms habitual movement patterns; excellent for chronic pain

name: Alexander Technique
note: Improves postural habits and movement efficiency; musicians, performers, and chronic pain populations

name: Polarity Therapy
note: Energy-based bodywork combining Ayurveda, osteopathy, and TCM concepts; hands-on and dietary guidance

name: Network Spinal Analysis
note: Light-touch spinal work facilitating nervous system reorganization; trauma and stress patterns

name: Bowen Therapy
note: Gentle cross-fiber muscle moves with pauses; resets nervous system tone; pain and stress relief

name: Zero Balancing
note: Combines structural and energy approaches; held fulcrums at bone and joint level; deep relaxation

name: Watsu
note: Shiatsu-based bodywork in warm pool; profound relaxation; trauma, pain, and somatic release

name: Biocharging PEMF Therapy
note: Pulsed electromagnetic fields at low frequencies; mitochondrial, bone, and pain applications

name: Laser Therapy Low Level
note: Therapeutic laser at non-heating wavelengths; wound healing, pain, and tissue repair

name: Dry Needling
note: Acupuncture-style needles into trigger points by physical therapists; muscle pain and spasm

name: Prolotherapy Ozone
note: Ozone combined with proliferant injected into joints; reported superior to dextrose prolotherapy by some practitioners

name: Functional Nutritionist Consultation
note: Personalized nutrition protocol based on labs, symptoms, and health history; functional and integrative approach

name: Health Coach Visit
note: Behavioral support for lifestyle change; nutrition, sleep, stress, and movement coaching

name: Infrared Photobiomodulation
note: Combination of red (630-700nm) and near-infrared (800-1000nm) wavelengths; broad therapeutic applications

name: SCENAR Therapy
note: Biofeedback device sending electrical impulses to tissues; pain and neurological applications

name: Frequency Specific Microcurrent
note: Low-amperage electrical current at specific frequencies for tissue healing; nerve and fibromyalgia applications

name: Myotherapy
note: Deep trigger point treatment following muscle origin/insertion chains; pain and tension patterns

name: Visceral Manipulation
note: Gentle manual therapy to restore mobility of organs and visceral fascia; digestive and structural applications

name: Neural Therapy
note: Injection of local anesthetic into scars, ganglia, or trigger areas to reset autonomic nervous system

name: Functional Lab Review
note: Detailed review of functional and standard labs with practitioner to identify patterns and root causes

name: Detox Protocol Supervision
note: Supervised detoxification program; may include sauna, supplements, diet modification, and drainage support

name: Mold Illness Treatment Protocol
note: Structured protocol for chronic inflammatory response syndrome from mold; cholestyramine, CSM, VIP phases

name: Lyme Integrative Protocol
note: Combination of antimicrobials, immune support, and detox for tick-borne illness; integrative approach
